title: Gendos feelings or lack thereof
wikitext:
[]: Gendo's feelings (or lack thereof)             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Sat Sep 22, 2007 1:15 am I didnt see this thread about this, so I decided to take a stab at it. Sory if it's been over done. Gendo Ikari. Possibly the worst father since Darth Vader or the Green Goblin. Satan made celluloid. Gendo represents all that is wrong and dark about humanity. He is selfish, manipulative and cruel. The entire series of events in Eva were all cleverly orecestrated from this man. He was the puppet master. He endangered the lives of countless people and was willing to sacrifce every single one of them for his own personal goal. Yui. His long lost wife. Now, most people belive that his reasons were for love. He loved Yui and was willing to sacrifce anything to be with her again. That's how most people his motives. Love is a funny thing. It can cause some people to do things others would find dangerous or unethical, if it were to benifeit the one you love. However with someone like Gendo, It makes me wonder if love was his true motive. I guess what I'm getting at is my ultimate question. Did Gendo love Yui? Is someone like Gendo CAPABLE of love? My take on it is this. I truely think that Gendo believed himself to love Yui. But his actions dont speak like a man in love. I belive if they were, he would have respected Yui's wishes. To me it seemed Gendo was acting as someone trying to reclaim a possesion, rather than to be reunited with a loved one. Like a child with a toy. He felt Yui was "his" and wanted her back, not because it was what she wanted or because of his love for her, but because having Yui with him made him feel good. In the end, I dont believe Gendo's actions were acts of love. What's everyone elses take on this?

[]: Re: Gendo's feelings (or lack thereof)             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Sep 22, 2007 1:32 am shinesman_cobalt wrote: But his actions dont speak like a man in love. I belive if they were, he would have respected Yui's wishes.

[]: Re: Gendo's feelings (or lack thereof)             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Sat Sep 22, 2007 1:40 am Ornette wrote: shinesman_cobalt wrote: But his actions dont speak like a man in love. I belive if they were, he would have respected Yui's wishes.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Sep 22, 2007 2:43 am Gendo is perhaps the most misunderstood character in the series. It's easy to hate him and not easy to understand him. Even the way he acted towards Shinji was out of love because he believed that he could only hurt Shinji. It's clear he was utterly lost without Yui and completely out of the loop as to her plans.

[]:             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Sat Sep 22, 2007 2:49 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Gendo is perhaps the most misunderstood character in the series. It's easy to hate him and not easy to understand him. Even the way he acted towards Shinji was out of love because he believed that he could only hurt Shinji. It's clear he was utterly lost without Yui and completely out of the loop as to her plans.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Sep 22, 2007 2:58 am He says so himself: ep26' wrote: Gendo: I've been waiting for this moment for so long... To finally be with you again, Yui. When I'm with Shinji, I only hurt him. So, it's better that I do nothing. Yui: So, you were afraid of Shinji. Gendo: I don't believe that I can be loved by others... I'm not worthy of love Kaworu: You're just running away. You simply reject the world before you get hurt. .

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sat Sep 22, 2007 7:54 am Ornette wrote: He says so himself: ep26' wrote: Gendo: I've been waiting for this moment for so long... To finally be with you again, Yui. When I'm with Shinji, I only hurt him. So, it's better that I do nothing. Yui: So, you were afraid of Shinji. Gendo: I don't believe that I can be loved by others... I'm not worthy of love Kaworu: You're just running away. You simply reject the world before you get hurt.

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Sat Sep 22, 2007 8:20 am When Gendo lost Yui, he couldn't handle being near Shinji. It caused him too much pain. However, I believe that when Gendo asked for Shinji for forgiveness, it showed that Gendo loved Shinji. He just couldn't show it because of his love for Yui. Gendo's "death" scene was one of my favorite scenes from EOE.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sat Sep 22, 2007 1:53 pm There's a surprising amount of Gendo-Shinji parallels, to be sure. Gendo never truly hated Shinji. If anything, he hated himself for mistreating him- which, of course, led him to further distance himself from his son, which, ironically, only made things worse. Notice how Gendo's only non-Rei-related loss-of-composure moment is when Shinji refuses to fight Bardiel.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Sep 22, 2007 1:56 pm What I love most about that scene in EoE, as well as a scene in Episode 21' ("I'm not used to being liked by others" *Cut of Gendou holding his hands in a similar way Shinji did once*), is that the Gendou-Shinji parallels are really driven home. Both feel that they can't be worthy of love and BOTH actually say in EoE "It was better when I did nothing." (I also think EoTV's "Thank you to my Father" as a reply to EoE's "I'm sorry, Shinji." is a very nice touch.) In terms of Gendou and Yui, obviously he loved her otherwise he wouldn't have tried to bring about his own version of Instrumentality to see her again. Keep in mind, he had no knowledge of Yui intentionally going into Eva, seeing as she confided in Fuyutsuki about this as opposed to Gendou. Personally, I think if Gendou knew about this, he wouldn't have allowed Yui to do that, though that would bring danger to them all due to SEELE. Of course, Gendou stepped on people just to get his way, such as sleeping with the Akagi women (Naoko and Ritsuko) and generally using them for their intelligence, which I guess can cause some fans to think he didn't truly "love" Yui... One thing that I wonder though, if Fuyutsuki would have somehow let it slip to Gendou that Yui purposely took part of the CE, how would he have reacted? They seem to be somewhat friends, though I'm certain how close... Would he have been so extreme as to order Fuyutsuki to be killed or just reprimand him in some way since he probably needed the Vice-Commander or what?

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sat Sep 22, 2007 1:58 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: What I love most about that scene in EoE, as well as a scene in Episode 21' ("I'm not used to being liked by others" *Cut of Gendou holding his hands in a similar way Shinji did once*), is that the Gendou-Shinji parallels are really driven home.

[]:             Quote Post by Incisivis » Sat Sep 22, 2007 3:21 pm Gendo did love Yui, I think. It would have been better if the series had shown that "other side" which Yui claims to see that no one else has, and how exactly he changed after Yui's death. But there doesn't seem to be anything to refute that Gendo loved Yui. Where Gendo fails is that he put all his effort into returning to Yui, and ignored and manipulated the people who still existed in human form. No matter how strong his love for one person was, that's still a major strike against him.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Sep 22, 2007 3:25 pm To be fair Yui was the first person to show him any kindness. To abruptly lose her like that had to have been incredibly tramatic for him. It's no real surprise he was so obsessed with getting her back.

[]:             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Sun Sep 23, 2007 4:23 am CitizenGeek wrote: Ornette wrote: He says so himself: ep26' wrote: Gendo: I've been waiting for this moment for so long... To finally be with you again, Yui. When I'm with Shinji, I only hurt him. So, it's better that I do nothing.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Sep 23, 2007 8:39 am Careful with the multi-quote boxes or Tines-ojichan will bite you. :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Sep 23, 2007 9:45 am shinesman_cobalt wrote: But... But... for Gendo to be Shinji like, that would mean that he's... human .

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Sep 23, 2007 3:13 pm shinesman_cobalt wrote: But... But... for Gendo to be Shinji like, that would mean that he's... human .

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Sep 23, 2007 8:47 pm Gendo represents human weakness for me, more so then even Shinji is a 14 year old boy that hasn't had any kind of emotional support since he was 4. Gendo is a man an adult, and a father. Worse off he know Yui the entire reason he does what he does to be with his wife again not be alone any more and have that affection from her. Ive said it a million times having the life he had and been married to yui he KNEW just how important affection was and because of simple fear he ran from his own child. The man was a coward a pitiful small human that is in reality worse then Shinji ever was. End Of Evangelion included.

[]:             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Sun Sep 23, 2007 8:55 pm BobBQ wrote: shinesman_cobalt wrote: But... But... for Gendo to be Shinji like, that would mean that he's... human .

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Sep 23, 2007 9:00 pm That's what makes small instances like Gendo trying to rescue Rei or arranging for the Entry Plugs to be inserted during the power blackout (Doing some of the pulling himself) really revealing. He does have feelings of care, but he simply buries them most of the time. IMO, refusing to care when you actually do is worse than not caring at all.
